name: Corrientes (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the colours of the Argentine national flag
the colours of the Argentine national flag
the Sun of May and the rays of the provincial coat of arms
the Phrygian cap of liberty in the coat of arms
the pike of the coat of arms

description: A horizontal triband of light blue, white and light blue, charged in the centre with the provincial coat of arms — clasped hands raising a Phrygian-capped pike before a Sun of May, framed by laurel branches.
history: Corrientes uses a flag in the colours of the Argentine national flag with the provincial coat of arms in the centre. The coat of arms, dating from the early nineteenth century, recalls the province's long-standing federalist and republican traditions.

features.symbolism: the colours of the Argentine national flag

features.role: coat of arms of Corrientes Province
features.symbolism: two clasped hands raising a pike topped by a red Phrygian cap of liberty before a radiant Sun of May, framed by laurel branches — the federal and republican emblems shared with the Argentine national arms
